---
title: "HEI and NAIF data changelog"
description: "Reconstructed change history for the shared `hei.csv` used by the NAIF and Swiss HEI dashboards, including reversals and corrections."
toc: false
draft: false
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
import sys
import pandas as pd

from IPython.display import Markdown, display

sys.path.insert(0, ".")

from _common import DATA_DIR, format_iso_date, latest_hei_changelog_date, load_hei_changelog

entries = load_hei_changelog()
latest_update = latest_hei_changelog_date()
current_row_count = len(pd.read_csv(DATA_DIR / "hei.csv"))
```

This page tracks the shared `hei.csv` baseline used by:

- [DORA & CoARA in Swiss Higher Education](naif/index.qmd)
- [Swiss HEI Open Data Dashboard](swiss-hei-open-data/index.qmd)

Downloads:

- [CSV](_data/hei.csv)
- [XLSX](_data/hei.xlsx)

::: {.callout-note}
This changelog is reconstructed from the git history of `dashboard/hei.csv`, `dashboards/naif/hei.csv`,
and `dashboards/_data/hei.csv`.

Several early commits, especially on 17 February 2026, used generic messages. Their summaries below are
therefore based on row-by-row CSV diffs rather than on detailed contemporaneous notes. When a later commit
clearly reversed or corrected an earlier change, the changelog says so explicitly.
:::

```{python}
#| echo: false
overview = "\n".join(
    [
        f"- **Latest recorded change:** {latest_update}",
        f"- **Current published rows:** {current_row_count}",
        f"- **Reconstructed entries:** {len(entries)}",
    ]
)
display(Markdown(overview))
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
repo_url = "https://github.com/eth-library/naif"

sections: list[str] = []
for entry in entries:
    sections.append(f"## {format_iso_date(entry['date'])}")
    sections.append(f"**Scope:** {entry['scope']}")

    row_count_before = entry.get("row_count_before")
    row_count_after = entry.get("row_count_after")
    if row_count_before is None:
        row_count_text = f"n/a -> {row_count_after} rows"
    else:
        row_count_text = f"{row_count_before} -> {row_count_after} rows"
    sections.append(f"**Row count:** {row_count_text}")
    sections.append(f"**Summary:** {entry['title']}")
    sections.append("")
    sections.append(entry["summary"])
    sections.append("")
    sections.append("### What changed")
    for item in entry["changes"]:
        sections.append(f"- {item}")

    corrections = entry.get("corrections", [])
    if corrections:
        sections.append("")
        sections.append("### Corrections and caveats")
        for item in corrections:
            sections.append(f"- {item}")

    sections.append("")
    sections.append("### Git provenance")
    for commit in entry["commits"]:
        short_sha = commit["sha"][:7]
        commit_url = f"{repo_url}/commit/{commit['sha']}"
        sections.append(f"- [`{short_sha}`]({commit_url}) {commit['message']}")

    sections.append("")

display(Markdown("\n".join(sections)))
```
